resources.string: Install the missing PS Vita firmware components so that apps can function correctly.
Create a local Vita user profile for saves, play history, and app data.
Are you sure you want to delete user \"%1$s\"?\n\nAll user data will be lost.
Create a user to start storing saves and play history.

plurals.name: batch_delete_message
plurals.item: Are you sure you want to delete %1$d selected application?\n\nThis action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to delete %1$d selected applications?\n\nThis action cannot be undone.

resources.string: This will permanently delete %1$s for this application. This action cannot be undone.
Are you sure you want to reset the play time for %1$s?
Vita3K does not condone piracy. You must dump your own games.
An update check is already running.

A newer Vita3K build is available.

Current version: %1$s
Latest version: %2$s

A newer Vita3K build is available.

Current version: %1$s
Latest version: %2$s
Published: %3$s

You are currently running a non-official or PR build.

If you update, you will switch to the latest official build and will no longer be on this custom or PR build.

Latest official build: %1$s

You are currently running a non-official or PR build.

If you update, you will switch to the latest official build and will no longer be on this custom or PR build.

Latest official build: %1$s
Published: %2$s

Vita3K is an open-source PlayStation Vita emulator written in C++ for Windows, Linux, macOS, and Android. The emulator is still under active development, so feedback and testing are greatly appreciated.
Vita3K does not condone piracy. You must dump your own games.
To get started, install the PS Vita firmware files below.
Any unsaved in-game progress will be lost.
This game has a saved custom config.
This game is using global settings.
Some settings take effect after restarting the game.
Some changes need an app restart to fully take effect.
These changed settings will apply after restart:\n- %1$s
Please wait while Vita3K restarts the game.
Keeps content installs running while Vita3K is backgrounded.
Choose a single archive file or a folder containing multiple .zip and .vpk archives.
Choose a license file or enter a zRIF key manually.
No license was found automatically.\nHow would you like to provide it?
Failed to create license from zRIF key.
The selected folder contains no .zip or .vpk archives.
Using custom config for this app
This will remove the custom configuration for %1$s and revert to global settings.
This will remove all per-app custom configuration files. This action cannot be undone.
You have unsaved changes. What would you like to do?
This resets the current settings form to the emulator defaults. Storage folder changes are not included until you change them separately.
Automatically select modules to load. Recommended for most apps.
Select this mode to load modules automatically in addition to selected modules from the list on the right.
Only load the modules selected from the list. Advanced users only.
No modules available. Install firmware first.
Pick the PlayStation firmware page locale to open in your browser.
Enable Dynarmic JIT optimizations. Improves performance.
Select the preferred backend renderer. Vulkan is recommended for most systems.
Set the renderer accuracy level for Vulkan. High accuracy may improve visuals but reduce performance.
Enable V-Sync for OpenGL. Reduces screen tearing.
Speed hack, disabling turns off surface syncing between CPU and GPU.\nSurface syncing is needed by a few games.\nGives a big performance boost if disabled (in particular when upscaling is on).
Allow pipelines to be compiled concurrently on multiple concurrent threads.\nThis decreases pipeline compilation stutter at the cost of temporary graphical glitches.
Select the final image filter to apply.
Scale the games resolution by a multiplier.\nExperimental: apps are not guaranteed to render properly at more than 1x.
Technique to increase the sharpness of textures which are sloped relative to the viewer.\nIt has no drawbacks but can impact performance on older GPUs.
Export textures used by the app to the textures folder.
Import replacement textures from the textures folder.
Game hack. May double the framerate from 30 FPS to 60 FPS in some games,\nbut can cause some games to run twice as fast.
Provides a way to force the GPU to run at the maximum possible clocks. Thermal constraints will still be applied.
Enable shader caching. Reduces stuttering on subsequent runs.
Pass generated Spir-V shader directly to driver.\nNote that some beneficial extensions will be disabled, and not all GPUs are compatible with this.
Memory mapping improved performance, reduces memory usage and fixes many graphical issues. However, it may be unstable on some GPUs.
Choose which installed custom GPU driver to inject. Installing drivers requires a compatible Android GPU driver ZIP and a supported Qualcomm Vulkan stack.
The selected custom driver is no longer installed. Choose Default, reinstall it, or select another installed driver before launching an app.
The selected custom driver is loaded and active.
The selected custom driver could not be loaded, so Vita3K is using the default system driver instead.
No memory mapping support for current device.
Failed to resolve custom driver archive path.
Installed custom driver %1$s, but it could not be loaded. Vita3K fell back to the default system driver.
Failed to install the selected custom driver archive.
Failed to load custom driver %1$s. Vita3K fell back to the default system driver.
This will permanently remove the installed custom driver %1$s from device storage.
Select the audio backend.\nCubeb is recommended for most systems.
Enable advanced audio library NGS support.
Select the front camera source. Solid Color or Static Image can be used as substitutes.
Select the back camera source. Solid Color or Static Image can be used as substitutes.
Choose the fallback color used when the camera source is Solid Color.
Pick the fallback image used when the camera source is Static Image.
Failed to resolve the selected camera image path.
Select which button acts as the Enter/Confirm button. Some apps ignore this setting.
Set the system language reported to applications.
Set the date format used by the emulated system.
Set the time format used by the emulated system.
Select which IME keyboard languages are available to applications.
Pretend to be signed in to PlayStation Network (but offline).
Enable HTTP networking support for apps that use it.
How many times to retry connecting when the server doesn\'t respond. Could be useful if you have very unstable or VERY SLOW internet.
How long to wait between connection retries. Could be useful if you have very unstable or VERY SLOW internet.
How many retries to attempt when there isn\'t more data to read, lower can improve performance but can make games unstable if you have bad enough internet.
How long to wait between read attempt retries, lower can improve performance but can make games unstable if you have bad enough internet
Select the IP address to be used in adhoc networking.
Show the touch control overlay while a game is running.
Hide Overlay When Controller Is Connected
Automatically hide the touch overlay whenever a physical controller is connected.
Add the extra trigger buttons to the touch overlay.
Add the stick-click buttons to the touch overlay.
Show the button that switches between front and rear touch input.
Show the button that temporarily hides the on-screen controls.
Resize the touch controls to better fit your hands and screen.
Adjust how transparent the touch controls appear over the game.
Shows the physical controllers Android currently reports to the frontend. Button labels follow the first connected controller when possible.
You can still choose mappings manually even if no controller is currently connected.
Choose which physical controller button is used for each input.
Tap a row, then press a controller button or choose one manually from the list.
Choose which controller axes drive the analog sticks and PSTV triggers.
Tap a row, then move a stick or trigger or choose an axis manually from the list.
Up and Down share one vertical axis. Left and Right share one horizontal axis.
Scale analog stick input before it reaches the emulator. Higher values make sticks feel more aggressive.
Ignore controller and device motion sensors for games that expect gyro or accelerometer input.
Press a controller button now, or choose a mapping from the list below.
Move a controller stick or trigger now, or choose a mapping from the list below.
Connect a controller to use live capture.
Enable Vulkan validation layers.\nUseful for debugging but reduces performance.
Enable the texture cache. Improves performance in games at the cost of additional VRAM usage.
Select the interface language used by the Android frontend.\nUse System Default to follow the operating system language.
Change the location of the emulated system storage folder.\nIf you move to a different folder, move your existing data there manually.
Open Android’s share sheet for the current Vita3K log file so it can be sent to Discord or another app.
Failed to resolve the selected storage folder.
Failed to switch the emulator storage folder.
Stretch the display area to fill the entire window, ignoring the original aspect ratio.
Check the box to get a pixel perfect rendering with HD resolutions (1080p, 4K) in fullscreen on a 16:9 aspect ratio monitor at the price of slight cropping at the top and the bottom of the screen.
Add an artificial delay to file loading. This is required for some games that load files too quickly compared to real hardware (e.g., Silent Hill).
Show a hint when shaders are being compiled during gameplay.
Choose whether Vita3K checks for updates at startup.\n"Check" notifies you when a newer build is available, and "Don\'t Check" disables startup checks.
Keep a duplicate of the log file named after the current title when an app runs.
Select the verbosity of the logging system.
Show a performance overlay with FPS and other statistics.
Select the detail level for the performance overlay.
Select the location to display the performance overlay in.
Select image format to select screenshots in.
